name: linear-claude-skill
description: Manage Linear issues, projects, and teams
capabilities: Create, view, update, and close Linear issues via CLI or MCP
Manage project states and link projects to initiatives
Add comments and status updates to issues
Execute bulk operations on multiple issues
Create sub-issues and manage issue hierarchies
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a legitimate Linear integration skill. Static scanner flagged 137 patterns as potential issues, but evaluation reveals all are false positives or legitimate usage. The skill requires network access to communicate with Linear API and environment variable access for API key authentication, which is standard for API integrations. Documentation explicitly includes security best practices for credential handling using varlock.
valueStatement: This skill enables Claude to interact with Linear for issue tracking, project management, and team collaboration workflows directly from your development environment.
limitations: Requires a valid Linear API key for authentication
Some advanced features require MCP server configuration
Rate limits apply based on Linear API constraints
Complex bulk operations may require helper scripts

useCases.title: Development Team Workflow
useCases.description: Create and manage issues directly from Claude when discussing bugs or feature requests during coding sessions

useCases.title: Project Tracking
useCases.description: Update project status and link issues to initiatives without leaving your coding environment

useCases.title: Task Automation
useCases.description: Automate repetitive status updates and bulk issue management through Claude commands

promptTemplates.title: Create a New Issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new issue in Linear with title '{title}' and description '{description}' in the '{project}' project
promptTemplates.scenario: When you identify a new bug or feature request during development

promptTemplates.title: Update Issue Status
promptTemplates.prompt: Update the status of issue {issue_id} to {status} (use: Backlog, Todo, In Progress, Done, Cancelled)
promptTemplates.scenario: When you complete a task or need to move an issue through workflow

promptTemplates.title: Bulk Status Update
promptTemplates.prompt: Update these issues to Done: {issue_list} (e.g., ENG-101, ENG-102, ENG-103)
promptTemplates.scenario: When completing multiple related tasks after a coding session

promptTemplates.title: Create Project with Issues
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new project named '{project_name}' linked to initiative '{initiative}' and add these issues: {issue_titles}
promptTemplates.scenario: Set up a new project phase with initial issues

bestPractices: Always link issues to projects and projects to initiatives for proper traceability
Use MCP tools when available for most operations; use CLI as fallback
Keep your Linear API key secure using environment variables, never hardcode it
antiPatterns: Do not create issues in catch-all projects then move them later - create them in the correct project from the start
Do not expose your Linear API key in terminal output or Claude context
Avoid using deprecated community MCP servers - use the official Linear MCP server

faq.question: How do I get a Linear API key?
faq.answer: Go to Linear Settings > Security & access > Personal API keys. Create a new key starting with 'lin_api_' and add it to your environment variables.

faq.question: What tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports MCP tools (preferred), Linear CLI, and helper scripts using the @linear/sdk package.

faq.question: Why am I seeing 'MCP tools not available'?
faq.answer: If MCP is not available, the skill will automatically fall back to using the Linear CLI or helper scripts. You do not need to report this as a blocker.

faq.question: How do I update multiple issues at once?
faq.answer: Use the bulk status command: npx tsx scripts/linear-ops.ts status Done ENG-101 ENG-102 ENG-103

faq.question: Can I create sub-issues?
faq.answer: Yes, use: npx tsx scripts/linear-ops.ts create-sub-issue ENG-100 'Sub-task title' 'Description'

faq.question: How do I link a project to an initiative?
faq.answer: Use: npx tsx scripts/linear-ops.ts link-initiative 'Project Name' 'Initiative Name'
